items.claim: Conor McGregor's speed and agility at 145 pounds is the best version of him in his career.
items.quote: I personally think this is the best weight that we'll ever see Connor at in in his entire career is 145.

items.claim: Conor McGregor carries more power at 145 pounds because his opponents have smaller frames.
items.quote: if anything, he has more power at 145 lbs because these guys are a little bit smaller.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's primary defensive strength is neutralizing an opponent's back hand and back leg by hand-fighting.
items.quote: Connor takes those weapons away better than everybody as a southpaw.

items.claim: Conor McGregor is uncomfortable and less effective when forced to fight moving backward.
items.quote: when Connor's backed up, he is uncomfortable and so he can't land his same shots backing up.

items.claim: Conor McGregor lacks high-level defensive grappling skills off his back.
items.quote: That's not somebody who's seasoned off of his back.

items.claim: Conor McGregor's range-finding and rhythm are disrupted when fighting another southpaw because the hand-fighting dynamic changes.
items.quote: So now you start to see how even it becomes in the striking. Now he's backing up Connor.

items.claim: Max Holloway's best path to victory is forcing Conor McGregor to move backward.
items.quote: So the biggest key for Max is to get him moving back.
